report_metadata.report_title: CISA Shares Lessons Learned from an Incident Response Engagement

ip_addresses.evidence_snippet: C2 Server IP Address was observed in mid-July to early August 2024.
C2 Server IP Address was observed in mid-July to early August 2024.

file_hashes.evidence_snippet: China Chopper Web Shell was observed in mid-July to early August 2024.
Generic PHP Web Shell was observed in mid-July to early August 2024.
Stowaway Proxy Tool was observed in mid-July to early August 2024.

tools.name: Burp Suite Burp Scanner
tools.evidence_snippet: CISA detected this scanning activity by analyzing web logs and identifying signatures associated with the tool.

tools.name: RingQ antivirus defense evasion tool
tools.evidence_snippet: CISA discovered evidence of various files hosted on the C2 server, including numerous publicly available tools and scripts: RingQ antivirus defense evasion tool (RingQ.exe, RingQ.rar).

tools.name: IOX proxy tool
tools.evidence_snippet: CISA discovered evidence of various files hosted on the C2 server, including numerous publicly available tools and scripts: IOX proxy tool (iox.rar).

cves.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors exploited CVE 2024-36401 on two of the organization’s public-facing GeoServers.

ttps.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors performed active scanning to identify vulnerabilities they could use for initial access.
The cyber threat actors gained remote access to the victim’s network using a desktop behind a virtual private server (VPS).
The cyber threat actors exploited CVE 2024-36401 on two of the organization’s public-facing GeoServers.
The cyber threat actors used PowerShell to download a payload.
The cyber threat actors employed indirect command execution via web shells.

summary: CISA shares lessons learned from an incident response engagement, highlighting the importance of prompt patching, practicing incident response plans, and implementing logging and aggregating logs in a centralized out-of-band location.

timeline.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors gained initial access to GeoServer 1 on July 11, 2024.
